title: Talking about my life - Boulder, Colorado 11/21

transcript.text: I remember the Longmont race was the same day as the time trial around Gun Barrel.
It was, like, around this big, like, block on Gun Barrel.
And we went out there the day before because Chris Wary's parents, like,
Had everybody in the team go out there and pre-ride the course.
Because they took it, I took that really seriously.
I don't know, like, where he was for those first two races.
Maybe because I think they were, like, in Vail or something busy.
But they took it really seriously.
And he's like, yeah.
And instead of putting on your brakes, just, if you're drafting, just pull out into the wind, man.
And stuff.
And anyways, we did that race.
And, like, we won that time trial.
But our team was in first place.
And we won the time trial.
And then the officials said that our team was in second place overall.
So they made, like, a mistake.
So, like, the parents, I think it was, like, Chris Wary's parents got all, like, pissed off
And went and talked to them and had it fixed.
But that time trial was in the same exact course as our road race was the year before.
I remember that.
Because, like, we'd ride on this, like, there was this big parking lot area.
We'd ride on this straightaway and then take a right.
And I remember when I got to the right, like, I think it was Peter Lynch.
He was, like, crying.
He was like, eh, because he crashed.
And I recognized him because he had glasses on.
And he was a little kid.
At least I thought it was him because he ended up getting second.
Maybe it was some kid who looked like him.
But, yeah, and then we did, like, the race around National Bureau of Standards
Where they have the International Time Clock or the American Time Clock,
Which is, like, two blocks from my house.
So that was cool.
And that had, like, lots of climbing and descending and stuff.
That was a good course.
And it was well-suited to me because it had a really, like, steady climb,
Which I could do well on.
Oh, yeah, because I got fourth in the in-car race because that was a steady climb
That I could just ride away from kids instead of getting dropped at them
Because of the speed surges and all, like, the momentum that helps you
When you go up and down all the hills and stuff.
But, yeah, I ended up getting eighth, and that was happy enough.
That was happy for me.
We won the team category.
And so that was after fifth grade.
And then after fifth grade, like, during sixth grade, my parents,
We had been downhill skiing every year until then, every weekend,
Because then we bought this trailer outside, four miles outside of Breckenridge
In a trailer park called Tiger Run.
And we were downhill skiing every year, all year, you know,
Mainly at Copper Mountain Resort.
And I was on, like, the ski team.
I was on the red team.
And I can't remember how many years we did that.
Maybe it was, like, two years or something, two or three years.
But I wasn't, and I did the racing, but I wasn't very good
Because my time was just not very good because it was these, like,
It was these slalom courses, and it was just all about momentum, you know.
I skied it perfectly, but I didn't get the time because I was too light
To keep that momentum up going around those turns.
But I had a friend there who was Reed who was from Breckenridge,
And we'd, like, go off and ski together and stuff and, like, do these big jumps
Off these hay bales, you know, and stuff.
And then we'd do, like, group skiing with the groups and stuff.
But he was, like, the only kid I hung out with.
I remember, like, we had this one day, like, they just, it wasn't really all that fun for me
Because whenever we do this, like, the skiing with the big group,
We had to go really slowly.
Like, the teacher was, like, or the guide or whatever, the instructor was, like,
Just stay behind me.
And, like, I remember one day I was, like, fuck this.
We went down, it was, like, this really steep course, and I just attacked it,
And I, like, skied down really hard, like, carving really hard, like,
Thrashing the bumps, and I got to the bottom first,
And these other older kids came up to me, and they were, like, all pissed off at me.
Like, what the fuck are you doing?
You passed the teacher.
You're not supposed to do that.
What the fuck?
You think you're all hot shit?
And I was, like, I just want to go skiing, man.
And so I didn't do it the next year.
Maybe because I didn't express interest.
So then my parents, instead of doing that, my parents enrolled us into,
My brother and I into the Nordic ski team in Lake Eldora,
Lake Eldora Nordic ski team, LERT.
And it was, like, supposed to be better cross-training for bike racing.
And so instead of, on the weekends, instead of going skiing,
Which we did sometimes, but instead of doing that,
We would just show up on the ski races that were all over Colorado
That we would be driven out there on the, you know, the team van and stuff.
And that was really fun, man.
LERT was, like, a highlight of my life.
It was fun because every, like, every single day after school,
We'd jump in that van, and they'd take us up to Lake Eldora,
And we'd ski these, like, and, like, I'm a sixth grader,
So for our little kid, it was exciting because we'd ski these, like,
Labyrinth of, like, trails, like, all over the place outside of Lake Eldora.
And we'd do these, like, intervals all the time, these interval time trials.
And so they'd go around, and then you'd tell your time,
And it was, like, always competing with each other.
And it was fun, and a lot of kids who were on that team were also bike racers.
Like, Ryder Clifford was there.
He, like, got second twice and won the Red Singer one year.
He was second to Bobby Julek, and I think he said he was, like, friends with him,
And they would, like, hang out with him when we had a ski race in, like, Glenwood Springs.
Bobby Julek got third in the Tour de France, and he won, yeah.
But, uh, and then his sister, Casey Clifford,
Who, she was a grade above me, but she was only, like, two months older than me.
I think she was born in, like, September.
October, November, December.
So she was, like, three months older than me.
But she was in the grade above me.
And then, uh, some kid named Pete.
And I think he had, like, a younger sister, too,
Who ended up joining the team, like, the next year.
So he was, she was, like, really hot.
And, um, and then this other kid, Pete,
Who was two years older than me, same year, or age as Ryder,
Who went to Boulder High and Baseline with me.
Pete Vordenberg.
And he went to the Olympics twice.
He was, like, a good skier.
And I heard he was, I was hanging out with his cousin, actually,
A couple weeks ago.
And, uh, Moab, a friend of mine from high school,
Who, like, I just saw, who I hadn't seen in some high school.
And he was, like, yeah, he's my cousin.
I was, like, what?
No way.
But, um, and then some kid named Corey.
And then some kid named Chad Kipfer.
Chad didn't really like me, though, man.
Like, I was kind of, I remember he gave me this Indian burn.
I was, like, does it hurt?
And I was, like, no.
So he did it, like, really hard.
He's, like, it's got to hurt now.
And I'm, like, no.
But it did.
But, yeah, he didn't seem to really like me.
He was, like, a couple years older than me.
Everybody, all the kids on that were, like,
At least a couple years older than me.
And then it was me, who was, like, younger.
And then Wyndham, who was, like, even younger than me.
And then there's another kid named Eli Front.
Or Nikolai Front.
And I think he did the Red Zinger thing with Wyndham, too, in 85.
As part of the little kids race.
He got third.
But he didn't race after that.
I heard he got into, like, speed skating after that.
But he was pretty good.
But, um, once he got lost, and he was, like, freaking out.
He was, like, crying.
Like, where are they?
And we were, like, we're over here.
Just keep going.
But, uh, but, like, yeah, it seemed like the people on the team, like,
They made a big deal out of, like, they were young kids now.
Oh, my God, now there's young kids.
And I was, like, what are you talking about, dude?
I'm just, I'm just like you.
But I don't know.
I felt, I wasn't really.
Ryder Clifford was really nice.
He was, like, my friend.
But he was, like, the only kid who, like, really talked to me.
But, um, yeah, and I couldn't go to all the ski races either,
Which kind of pissed me off.
Like, they had a ski race and steamboat.
And I was, like, cool, man, ski race and steamboat.
Because those ski races on the weekend were fun, too.
Because then we'd show up, and it'd be, like, all the different teams
From all over the state would show up.
And you'd do the time trial, you know, staggered.
And you'd see what place you got.
And the kids on our team were pretty good.
Like, Ryder and Pete were good.
And some other kid were, that other kid, Pete, I think, was good.
And it was fun.
And I wasn't all that good, though.
Well, because my age group, we only got to do 5K.
And it was, like, rolling hills.
And I was just too light to be able to work up a speed and do well.
But in 10K, I did well.
I remember there was the Frisco Gold Rush.
And this was actually the year.
Yeah, it was that year we did the Frisco Gold Rush.
And I got third in junior category or whatever junior category my age group was in
To Andy Hooker, who was the same kid who won the Red Zinger in 84.
But he wasn't on the Lert team, so he must have just been a skier.
But he did join the Lert team the next year.
But, yeah, those races were fun because there was, like, so many people there
And just so much energy.
And the courses were, like, really exciting.
Like, the mountains look totally different in the winter than in the summer.
Like, in the summer, we did lots of biking because we had the trailer up in Breckenridge.
And so we rode around the path, the bike paths, all the time.
They had these 40-mile bike paths from Breckenridge to Vail that I'd ride all the time,
Like, with my parents and by myself and then with my brother.
And then the bike paths went around Lake Dillon and just everywhere.
And, but then in the winter, and then, you know, we'd downhill ski.
But then in the wintertime, and even mountain bike.
Well, actually, I started mountain biking in 87.
But then the wintertime would come, and we'd do the same stuff and trails and all this stuff.
But it would look totally different, and it was just really fun for a kid.
But, like, yeah, one time they were like, yeah, we have the, we have the, uh, the Steamboat race.
And I was like, cool, man, Steamboat race.
They were like, no, but it's only for the older kids.
You can't come.
And I was like, what?
But I, and I was like, Casey Clifford's doing it.
And she's like, she's one of the older kids.
And I didn't say anything, but I was thinking, what the fuck are you talking about?
She's three months older than I am, but whatever.
Maybe they had the demarcation line.
Maybe October was the demarcation line or something.
But, uh, anyways, um, that was fun.
Um, oh yeah, Sarah Daney, and her dad was, like, really involved.
He was, like, one of the guys who would, like, ski with us all the time.
And I think he was, like, a racer.
And she was a fast racer, too.
And then, um, who else?
Yeah.
Oh, Cynthia Bridges was on it for a little bit.
She won the Red Zinger.
She was a bike racer.
And then some kid, chick named Melanie Muth, who was also a bike racer, was on it for a little while.
And, uh, yeah.
But you pretty much could fit the entire team in the van.
In, like, this big van.
So it wasn't all that big.
But, um, so that was after fifth grade, or during sixth grade.
And then we, and then I did it again during seventh grade.
And, um, and that year, this kid Lorenzo Wurster joined the team.
And, um, he was pretty good.
And I guess he stuck with it.
After that, for a few more years.
And so he was, like, another younger kid who was on it.
Um, but yeah, I wasn't all that good.
Oh, yeah, there was this kid named, I can't remember what his name was, but, um, he was from Fort Collins.
And he'd come down and do the teaching with us.
And he was really good.
And he looked, like, little just like me.
But he was fast.
He was faster than I was.
And I think he'd been doing it longer than me.
Maybe that's why I wasn't all that good.
I remember once we did a practice run with him and some other kid in, like, steamboat.
And he, like, those guys blew me away on the downhill.
And I was, like, confused as to how they could have blown me away that fast.
Maybe it was because they were heavier than me.
I don't know.
But, um, but yeah, then we did it the next year.
And, uh, I did one, I did well in the Winter Park race once because it was a longer distance.
And I remember I almost beat Corey's time.
And he was, like, and he was, like, a couple years older than me.
And they were, like, whoa, nice job, dude.
You're good.
And I was, like, I was, like, yeah, well, give me more races that are longer.
Um, and my parents were at that one.
Sometimes my parents showed up at the races.
Um, and maybe even partook and skied around and stuff.
And, yeah, and then I did it in seventh grade.
But I only did it two years because I developed a knee disease after that.
And I was supposed to lay off of my knees.
But I think another reason my parents encouraged me not to do it was because, um, it was just expensive.
It was, like, 600 bucks a month, you know, with all the expenses per kid.
And, like, my brother, I was just, like, whatever, just let my brother do it.
And, um, I don't know.
I just felt kind of, like, a loner.
I didn't really feel like I got along with those kids very well anyways.
Anyways, um, and so my brother did it for, like, another three years or something and got pretty good.
He said he was getting, like, second place to, like, the Junior Olympics champion every year.
And he won his last race, but then they disqualified him for taking a wrong turn on accident.
But, oh, yeah, and then a bunch of other kids who bike raced joined it after I quit.
Like, JP, Dave Alonji, I think his sister Ingrid did, too.
Um, I think Ginny Guidry was on there, Jessica Whitehall.
And, um, and the year I was on it for a little bit, Kirsten Boshan was on it.
She was, she went to my junior high.
But she was only there for, like, and her brother was on it for longer than her.
And she was on it for a couple weeks, but then she, like, didn't like it, so she dropped out.
But, uh, yeah, so that was that.
So then the next year in the Red Zinger, I'll talk about the Red Zinger.
Ooh, I'm almost out of time.
I'm so glad that I caught myself, though.
Because I'm going to have to splice that other one.
That's going to take forever.
Mm-hmm.
Mm-hmm.
Mm-hmm.
